regional_requirements.description: EU member state requirements for audience scanning laser displays

Status_A.description: Audience scanning requires permit or explicit permission in advance

Status_C.description: Permit required but often not followed or enforced
Status_C.notes: Law requires permits but enforcement varies

Status_D.notes: No specific laws or regulations, general safety principles apply

Status_E.description: Not allowed under almost all circumstances
Status_E.notes: Strict prohibition on indoor audience scanning

Status_F.notes: Usually no permission required but authorities may deny case-by-case

EU_2006_25_EC.name: EU Directive 2006/25/EC
EU_2006_25_EC.title: Minimum health and safety requirements regarding worker exposure to artificial optical radiation
EU_2006_25_EC.applicability: Worker exposure to optical radiation in EU member states
key_provisions: Employers must assess and manage risks from artificial optical radiation
Exposure limit values (ELVs) must not be exceeded
Risk assessment required for all laser work environments

EN_60825.name: EN 60825
EN_60825.title: European adoption of IEC 60825
EN_60825.scope: Laser product safety standard across EU

CE_Marking.requirement: CE marking required for laser products sold in EU
CE_Marking.compliance: Must comply with applicable EU directives including 2006/25/EC
CE_Marking.notes: Member states may implement additional national requirements

finland.scanning_restrictions: Only grating effects typically allowed, no scanned effects without special permit

sweden.scan_fail_devices: PASS or equivalent required for scanned effects

switzerland.regulatory_body: Swiss Federal Office of Public Health (FOPH)
switzerland.notification_requirement: 14 days advance notice via online portal

competence_certificates.level_1: Events with no laser light in audience area (3m above floor or 2.5m lateral separation)
competence_certificates.level_2: Events with laser light in audience area - requires MPE calculation knowledge

scotland.local_authority_role: Environmental Health Officers review all submissions
typical_requirements: Statement of no audience scanning (often requested)
scotland.notes: Glasgow Council particularly strict - no exposure to diffuse reflections permitted

luxembourg.mpe_requirement: No radiation above MPE may touch living things including humans

page_analyses.title: Worldwide Audience Scanning Laws Overview
critical_points: ILDA members must affirm safety regardless of local laws
Pulsed lasers should never be used for audience scanning
No audience scanning without proper equipment and procedures

page_analyses.title: Country Classifications Continued
critical_points: Information compiled mid-2014 - may be outdated

page_analyses.title: Selected Country Details - Part 1
critical_points: Finland: No audience scanning allowed in standard regulations
France: Some regions prohibit all Class 4 laser devices

page_analyses.title: Selected Country Details - Part 2
critical_points: Scotland: Local authorities typically veto audience scanning
Netherlands: German TUV standards followed as rule of thumb

page_analyses.title: Selected Country Details - Part 3
critical_points: Sweden: All shows inspected by authorities

page_analyses.title: Switzerland Competence Requirements and UK Overview
critical_points: Swiss Level 2 requires MPE calculation knowledge and programming skills
UK: No prior written permission required if paperwork standards met

page_analyses.title: UK Practices Continued
critical_points: UK operators supply risk assessments, method statements, insurance copies
HSG guidance being updated with PLASA distribution
PASS and SafetyScan systems increasingly expected
